title: To Kill a Mockingbird
description: A young girl in a small Alabama town watches her father, a principled lawyer, defend a Black man falsely accused of a crime, and comes to understand prejudice, courage, and moral growth in her community.

title: The Great Gatsby
description: A mysterious millionaire's obsessive pursuit of a lost love unfolds against the excess of the Jazz Age, exposing the hollowness beneath the American Dream.

title: Beloved
description: A formerly enslaved woman is haunted, literally and figuratively, by the trauma of her past when a mysterious young woman arrives at her home.

title: The Kite Runner
description: A boy's betrayal of his closest friend in Kabul haunts him into adulthood, driving a journey of guilt, war, and an attempt at redemption decades later.

title: Dune
description: On a desert planet that is the sole source of a universe-altering substance, a young heir becomes entangled in prophecy, ecology, and interstellar politics.

title: Project Hail Mary
description: A lone astronaut wakes with no memory on a solo mission to save Earth from an extinction-level threat, and must solve science-driven puzzles to survive.

title: Neuromancer
description: A washed-up computer hacker is hired for one last job that pulls him into a dangerous conspiracy involving artificial intelligence and corporate power.

title: Ender's Game
description: A gifted child is trained in a brutal military academy to command Earth's fleet against an alien threat, at steep personal and moral cost.

title: The Hobbit
description: A reluctant, home-loving hobbit is swept into a quest with a band of dwarves to reclaim treasure guarded by a dragon, discovering unexpected courage along the way.

title: A Game of Thrones
description: Noble houses vie for control of a fractured kingdom while an ancient threat stirs beyond a great northern wall, in a story of politics, betrayal, and war.

title: The Name of the Wind
description: A legendary figure recounts his own life story, from a childhood in a traveling troupe to his years of poverty and study at a university of magic.

title: Circe
description: A minor goddess exiled for her unruly power comes into her own on a lonely island, crossing paths with figures from Greek myth and forging her own fate.

title: Gone Girl
description: When a woman vanishes on her wedding anniversary, suspicion falls on her husband, and the unraveling truth reveals a marriage built on deception.

title: The Silent Patient
description: A woman stops speaking after allegedly murdering her husband, and the psychotherapist determined to treat her uncovers a shocking hidden motive.

title: The Da Vinci Code
description: A symbologist races across Europe to decode clues hidden in famous artworks, unraveling a centuries-old secret tied to a powerful religious mystery.

title: In the Woods
description: A detective investigating a child's murder in his hometown must confront the unsolved disappearance of his own childhood friends decades earlier.

title: Outlander
description: A World War II nurse is mysteriously transported to 18th-century Scotland, where she is torn between two centuries and two very different loves.

title: Me Before You
description: A young woman hired to care for a paralyzed former businessman forms an unexpected bond with him that challenges both their views on how to live.

title: The Hating Game
description: Two rival co-workers locked in a relentless office feud discover their antagonism is masking a very different kind of tension.

title: It Ends with Us
description: A young woman's whirlwind romance forces her to confront patterns of abuse she witnessed in her own childhood and to make a difficult choice about her future.

title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
description: A sweeping account of how Homo sapiens came to dominate the planet, tracing the cognitive, agricultural, and scientific revolutions that shaped civilization.

title: A Brief History of Time
description: A physicist explains the origins of the universe, black holes, and the nature of time and space in terms accessible to general readers.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
description: A Nobel laureate explains the two systems that drive human thought — fast, intuitive judgment and slow, deliberate reasoning — and where each fails us.

title: Atomic Habits
subtitle: An Easy & Proven Way to Build Good Habits & Break Bad Ones
description: A practical guide to building better habits through small, consistent changes, grounded in behavioral science and real-world examples.

title: The Diary of a Young Girl
description: The personal diary of a Jewish teenager hiding with her family in Amsterdam during the Nazi occupation, offering a firsthand account of hope amid horror.

title: Guns, Germs, and Steel
subtitle: The Fates of Human Societies
description: An exploration of why some societies developed technology and empire faster than others, argued through geography, agriculture, and disease rather than race.

title: Steve Jobs
description: An authorized biography of Apple's co-founder, drawing on extensive interviews to portray his creative vision, perfectionism, and complicated personal life.

title: Unbroken
subtitle: A World War II Story of Survival, Resilience, and Redemption
description: An Olympic runner turned WWII airman survives a plane crash, weeks adrift at sea, and years in brutal prisoner-of-war camps in a story of endurance.

title: How to Win Friends and Influence People
description: A foundational guide to building rapport, handling people diplomatically, and communicating persuasively in personal and professional relationships.

title: Rich Dad Poor Dad
description: Contrasting lessons from two father figures illustrate different attitudes toward money, assets, and financial independence.

title: The Lean Startup
description: A methodology for building startups through rapid experimentation, validated learning, and iterative product development rather than big upfront planning.

title: Grit
subtitle: The Power of Passion and Perseverance
description: A psychologist argues that sustained passion and perseverance, more than talent, predict long-term achievement, drawing on research and case studies.

title: The Fault in Our Stars
description: Two teenagers who meet at a cancer support group fall in love while grappling with mortality, meaning, and what it means to leave a mark on the world.

title: The Hunger Games
description: In a totalitarian nation, a teenage girl volunteers to take her sister's place in a televised fight to the death, sparking rebellion against the ruling elite.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
description: An orphaned boy discovers he is a wizard on his eleventh birthday and enrolls in a magical school, where he begins uncovering the truth about his past.

title: Percy Jackson and the Lightning Thief
description: A boy discovers he is the son of a Greek god and is drawn into a quest across modern America to prevent a war among the gods of Olympus.

title: Charlotte's Web
description: A spider befriends a young pig destined for slaughter and uses clever wordplay in her web to save his life, a story of friendship and sacrifice.

title: Where the Wild Things Are
description: A misbehaving boy sails to an island of monstrous creatures who crown him their king, before he chooses to return home to where he is loved.

title: Matilda
description: A brilliant, neglected girl with a gift for telepathy uses her wits and hidden powers to stand up to her cruel family and a tyrannical headmistress.

title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar
description: A tiny caterpillar eats its way through a week of different foods before transforming into a butterfly, in a simple story about growth and change.

title: It
description: A group of childhood friends reunite as adults to confront a shape-shifting entity that preys on children in their hometown, decades after their first encounter.

title: Dracula
description: A Transylvanian count's plan to spread his curse to England is opposed by a small group who must learn his supernatural weaknesses to stop him.

title: The Shining
description: A writer takes a winter caretaking job at an isolated, haunted hotel, where supernatural forces exploit his personal demons and endanger his family.

title: Frankenstein
subtitle: Or, The Modern Prometheus
description: A scientist's creation of an artificial being ends in tragedy, raising early questions about ambition, responsibility, and what makes someone monstrous.

title: Pride and Prejudice
description: A sharp-witted young woman navigates courtship, family expectations, and her own pride and misjudgments as she comes to reconsider a proud suitor.
In a totalitarian superstate under constant surveillance, a low-level worker begins to question the regime's control over truth, language, and thought.

title: War and Peace
description: Interweaving the lives of several Russian aristocratic families during the Napoleonic Wars, the novel explores love, fate, and the meaning of history.

title: Leaves of Grass
description: A landmark poetry collection celebrating individuality, democracy, nature, and the human body in free verse that broke from formal poetic convention.

title: The Odyssey
description: After the fall of Troy, a Greek king spends ten years journeying home, facing monsters, gods, and temptation while his wife fends off suitors.

title: Crime and Punishment
description: A destitute former student murders a pawnbroker and grapples with guilt, justification, and redemption in Tsarist-era St. Petersburg.

title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
description: Seven generations of the Buendía family live out love, war, and prophecy in the mythical town of Macondo, blending myth and history into one saga.

title: Educated
subtitle: A Memoir
description: Raised by survivalist parents in rural Idaho with no formal schooling, the author claws her way to a Cambridge PhD while reckoning with family and identity.

title: Born a Crime
subtitle: Stories from a South African Childhood
description: Comedian Trevor Noah recounts growing up mixed-race under apartheid South Africa, where his very existence was illegal, through humor and hardship.

title: Long Walk to Freedom
description: Nelson Mandela recounts his journey from rural childhood to 27 years of imprisonment to becoming South Africa's first democratically elected president.

title: Cosmos
description: A sweeping tour of the universe, from the origins of life on Earth to the possibility of extraterrestrial civilizations, told with wonder and rigor.

title: The Selfish Gene
description: A gene-centered view of evolution argues that genes, not individuals or species, are the primary unit of natural selection driving behavior.

title: Silent Spring
description: A landmark exposé on the environmental damage caused by pesticides, credited with launching the modern environmental movement.

title: Man's Search for Meaning
description: A psychiatrist and Holocaust survivor argues that finding meaning, even in the worst suffering, is what allows people to endure and persevere.

title: The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck
description: A counterintuitive self-help book arguing that a good life comes from caring about fewer, better things instead of chasing constant positivity.

title: Deep Work
subtitle: Rules for Focused Success in a Distracted World
description: An argument for the value of focused, undistracted work in a world of constant interruption, with practical strategies for cultivating deep focus.

title: Clean Code
subtitle: A Handbook of Agile Software Craftsmanship
description: A guide to writing readable, maintainable code through naming, functions, and design principles, illustrated with refactoring case studies.

title: Introduction to Algorithms
description: A comprehensive, rigorous textbook covering algorithm design, analysis, and data structures, widely used in university computer science courses.

title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
description: A deep dive into the principles behind reliable, scalable, and maintainable data systems, covering databases, streaming, and distributed systems.

title: The Notebook
description: A poor young man and a wealthy young woman fall in love one summer, only to be separated by class and circumstance, with a devoted reunion decades later.

title: Beach Read
description: Two rival writers with writer's block, one a cynic and one a romantic, swap genres for the summer and end up rewriting their assumptions about love.

title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
description: A disgraced journalist and a brilliant hacker investigate a decades-old disappearance, uncovering a trail of corporate corruption and violence.

title: The Silence of the Lambs
description: A young FBI trainee seeks the counsel of an imprisoned cannibalistic psychiatrist to catch a serial killer skinning his female victims.

title: Mistborn: The Final Empire
description: In a world where ash falls from the sky and an immortal emperor rules through terror, a street urchin with rare magical powers joins a heist to topple him.

title: The Left Hand of Darkness
description: An envoy from an interstellar alliance navigates the politics of a wintry planet whose inhabitants have no fixed gender, challenging his assumptions.

title: Foundation
description: A mathematician predicts the fall of a galactic empire and establishes a foundation to preserve knowledge and shorten the coming dark age.

title: The Haunting of Hill House
description: Four strangers investigating paranormal activity at a notoriously haunted mansion find the house's influence preying on their sanity, one by one.

title: Rosemary's Baby
description: A young wife in Manhattan grows suspicious that her eccentric neighbors and her own husband are part of a satanic plot involving her unborn child.

title: The Giving Tree
description: A tree gives everything it has, again and again, to a boy across his lifetime, in a spare parable about generosity and selflessness.

title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid
description: A middle-schooler's illustrated diary chronicles the awkward, funny mishaps of surviving school, friendships, and family with wry self-deprecation.

title: The Chronicles of Narnia: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
description: Four siblings step through a magical wardrobe into the snowbound land of Narnia, where they must help a lion king overthrow an eternal winter.

title: Zero to One
subtitle: Notes on Startups, or How to Build the Future
description: A contrarian guide to building breakthrough companies, arguing true progress comes from creating something new rather than copying what already works.

title: Good to Great
subtitle: Why Some Companies Make the Leap... and Others Don't
description: A research-driven study of companies that achieved sustained excellence, identifying leadership and discipline traits that separate good from great.

title: Divergent
description: In a future Chicago divided into rigid personality factions, a girl who doesn't fit any single category discovers a conspiracy threatening her world.

title: Six of Crows
description: A crew of six dangerous outcasts is recruited for a seemingly impossible heist that could make them rich — or get them all killed.

title: Salt, Fat, Acid, Heat
subtitle: Mastering the Elements of Good Cooking
description: A chef breaks cooking down into four foundational elements, teaching intuition over recipes so readers can cook confidently from any cuisine.

title: Into the Wild
description: A journalist reconstructs the final journey of a young man who gave away his savings and walked into the Alaskan wilderness, where he starved to death.

title: Maus
subtitle: A Survivor's Tale
description: A graphic memoir depicting the author's father's experience surviving the Holocaust, rendered with Jews as mice and Nazis as cats.

title: Watchmen
description: A murder investigation among retired vigilantes unravels a conspiracy that deconstructs the superhero genre against the backdrop of nuclear paranoia.

title: Things Fall Apart
description: A respected Igbo leader's world unravels as British colonialism and Christian missionaries disrupt the traditions and values of his village.
